summary: Iteration 3 focused on the 3 reported bugs + curated Indian audio presets on Emergent Object Storage. Backend pytest v3 (7 new preset tests + 25 v2 regression) = 32/32 pass. Frontend E2E via Playwright verified: (1) invitation delete now works via shadcn AlertDialog (data-testid=invite-delete-{id} -> invite-delete-confirm-{id}) and backend GET returns 404; (2) guest delete works via AlertDialog (remove-guest-{id} -> remove-guest-confirm-{id}); (3) save-flush behavior confirmed on BOTH share-button click (title typed then editor-share-btn clicked within 500ms, backend has new title, /i/{share} shows new title) AND on unmount via nav-dashboard-link (fetch keepalive persists new title, dashboard card reflects it after ~2.5s). Curated presets: GET /api/music-presets returns 7 items with correct fields (id,label,category,storage_path,content_type,size,created_at) — 4 generic (Ambient Piano/Elegant Strings/Cinematic/Uplifting @ audio/mpeg) + 3 Indian (Sitar (Raga Yaman)/Ceremonial Bell/Temple Bell @ audio/ogg with storage_path invitecraft/presets/audio/*.ogg). GET /api/files/{storage_path} for presets returns 200 with audio body WITHOUT any auth (public preset flag). Editor Media tab music-preset select lists all 7 backend presets + 'No music' (Indian ones decorated with ' · 🕉' suffix — cosmetic only, backend label unchanged). Selecting Temple Bell persists music_url='invitecraft/presets/audio/temple_bell.ogg' + music_label='Temple Bell'. Fresh no-auth browser context loading /i/{shareId} renders MusicPlayer with <audio src=/api/files/.../temple_bell.ogg> returning 200.
critical_code_review_comments: Editor.jsx: title input testid is 'input-title', not 'editor-title-input' as mentioned in review request — request wording mismatch, code is fine but keep consistent naming in future.

Indian preset labels are decorated with ' · 🕉' at render time in Editor.jsx; if a future test asserts exact-match on option text, it will fail — consider using a separate visual badge instead of mutating the label string.
server.py still ~1035 lines — refactor into routers/ modules recommended (carried over from iter 2).
download_file uses raw $regex on user-influenced path segments for the referenced-invitation check; low risk but re.escape would be safer (carried over from iter 2).

success_rate.frontend: 100% of the 6 targeted flows: delete-invite, delete-guest, flush-on-share, flush-on-unmount, preset-select-persistence, public-view-audio-200

seed_data_creation: None manual. Backend seeds the 7 curated presets on startup (idempotent) into db.music_presets + db.files with is_public_preset=true; seeding was already complete on entry.
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_v3_test.py = 4 preset tests (list has 7, indian public serving, generic public serving, no-auth requirement). Preset seeding runs in background at startup — if empty on first check, _wait_for_presets() polls for up to 40s. Frontend Editor route is /editor/:id (NOT /invite/:id/edit). Title input testid = 'input-title'. LocalStorage keys: 'ic_token' + 'ic_user'. To skip Templates flow, seed invitations via POST /api/invitations directly. Indian preset labels show with ' · 🕉' suffix in the dropdown; backend label is plain. audio_src on /i/{share} = full URL to /api/files/{storage_path} — public serve does not require ?auth=.
rca of the issue: N/A — all 3 reported bugs (delete invitation, delete guest, save not applied) verified fixed; new music-preset feature verified end-to-end.
